## RequestHook type

Async-capable request hook used by the built-in Pi WebSocket route and `protectWebSocketBehavior()`<!-- -->.

**Signature:**

```typescript
export type RequestHook<Session = unknown> = (
  request: AuthorizationRequest,
  context: RequestHookContext<Session>,
) => MaybePromise<RequestHookResult<Session>>;
```

**References:** [AuthorizationRequest](./pi-ws.authorizationrequest.md)<!-- -->, [RequestHookContext](./pi-ws.requesthookcontext.md)<!-- -->, [RequestHookResult](./pi-ws.requesthookresult.md)

## Remarks

Hooks receive the structured request plus a mutable shared context. They can deny the request, attach a `session`<!-- -->, or populate `locals` for later access.
